data.narrative:
Castelion is a Series B defense and security company founded by Bryon Hargis, Sean Pitt, and Andrew Kreitz, currently valued at $1.0B+ post-money following $100M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies—two publicly traded (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private firms at equivalent stage and sector positioning, including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Defense Unicorns, and Heven AeroTech.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach applied a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $1.0B last post-money valuation, yielding $1.9B. The peer-median method assessed five same-sector, same-stage comparables, which showed a median valuation of $1.4B with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B. This peer cohort directly anchors relative positioning within the defense tech Series B market.

The triangulated analysis implies a valuation range of $1.2B to $2.2B, with a midpoint of $1.6B. The spread reflects material uncertainty around stage-specific step-up rates and peer selection weighting. Key variables affecting terminal valuation include capital efficiency relative to peer benchmarks, customer concentration and contract durability, and the timing and terms of the next capital event.
